title: Portfolio / case-study showcase
description: Data contract for the portfolio format: a client-facing showcase of delivered work, rendered as an editorial hero + responsive case-card gallery + client wordmark chips + capability band + dark contact footer. Default theme: Cyan. The gallery of case cards is the page's hero surface; every card must carry real measured outcomes. The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

org.description: Organisation name (e.g. Independent Research). Shown in the slim brand bar wordmark.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label above the hero headline, e.g. 'Selected Work'.

title.description: Page title used in <title>. Typically 'Independent Research — Selected Work'.

subtitle.description: Optional browser subtitle or standfirst override.

date.description: ISO date the portfolio was produced (YYYY-MM-DD).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. Default for this format is 'cyan'.

hero.description: Editorial hero section (.showcase). Contains the oversized display headline, a standfirst paragraph, and an aggregate outcomes strip (.lead-stats). This is the first thing the reader sees; it should be bold and number-led.

eyebrow.description: Optional tracked-mono label above the headline, e.g. 'Delivered. Measured. Owned.'

headline.description: Oversized display headline (38–56px / 800). One short sentence; one word may be wrapped in <em> for the accent colour. Renders as .display.

standfirst.description: One paragraph (≤3 sentences) below the headline. Sets the 'why' for the page. Renders as .standfirst.

leadStats.description: 3–4 aggregate outcome figures displayed on the accent band below the standfirst (.lead-stats). These are portfolio-wide totals, not per-case numbers. Use tnum figures.

unit.description: Optional unit suffix appended in smaller type, e.g. '%', 'M', 'wks'.

label.description: Tracked-mono label below the figure, e.g. 'engagements delivered', 'cost reduction'. Keep ≤5 words.

cases.description: The case-card gallery (.gallery of .case-card). Every card must have real measured outcomes — no card without an outcome-row. The gallery is the focal point of the page. Cards render in array order. 4–9 cards is the comfortable range for a single-page scroll.

id.description: Stable slug used as the thumb index chip (e.g. 'case-01', 'proj-alpha').

title.description: Case / project title shown as .case-title (15–17px bold).

sectorTag.description: Mono pill tag shown above the title (ref · sector, e.g. '01 · Banking', '04 · InsurTech'). Renders as .case-tag.

role.description: Role / sector / year line below the title in mono micro (e.g. 'Lead Architect · Core Banking · FY24–25'). Renders as .case-role.

description.description: Optional 1–2 sentence summary of what was delivered. Keep concise; outcomes tell the real story. Renders as .case-desc.

thumbKind.description: CSS-art thumbnail variant class. Each card in a deck should use a different variant to ensure every thumb is visually distinct. All thumbs are palette-only CSS/SVG — no raster images.

outcomes.description: Measured outcome cells (.outcome-row). Mandatory: every card must have at least one outcome. 2–3 outcomes per card is ideal. Use tnum figures with tone where appropriate.

value.description: The tnum outcome figure, e.g. '−40%', '¥1.2B', '100%', '+18pp'. Use ok/risk/hold tone conventions honestly.

unit.description: Optional unit suffix in small type, e.g. '%', 'M'.

label.description: Short tracked-mono label below the figure (≤4 words), e.g. 'cost reduction', 'on-time filings'.

tone.description: Outcome number colour tone: 'ok' for positive wins (green/teal), 'risk' for honest negatives, 'hold' for caution, or omit for neutral (ink colour).

tags.description: Technology / method pill tags shown at the bottom of the card (.case-tags row). Rendered as mono micro pills. 2–5 tags is typical.

status.description: Optional status flag applied to the card as a .st-badge. Maps to 'ok' (completed / reference-verified), 'hold' (in progress), or 'risk' (at risk).

logos.description: Client wordmark chips (.logo-chip row). Rendered as styled text in rounded pills — never raster logos or images. Each entry is a client identity expressed purely as typography.

name.description: Client display name as it appears in the chip, e.g. 'ACME Financial', 'northwind bank', 'Vol·Tek'.

style.description: Optional rendering hint for the chip typographic treatment: 'bold' (default, all-caps heavy), 'italic' (italic light), 'tracked' (wide tracking mono), 'mixed' (mixed weight accent).

capabilities.description: Capability band cells (.capability-band › .capability). 4–6 cells on a --bg-band strip, each with an icon, a title, and a one-line description.

icon.description: House icon key from the icons/ catalog. Recommended categories for portfolio: editorial, data, people, commerce.

contact.description: Dark contact / footer band (.contact-band). Closes the page with a CTA, contact meta, and the org wordmark. Brand-neutral by default.

ctaEyebrow.description: Small mono eyebrow above the CTA headline, e.g. 'Start a conversation'.

ctaHeadline.description: CTA headline shown large in white (e.g. 'Have an outcome in mind? Let's scope it.').

ctaBody.description: One short paragraph supporting the CTA.

documentLink.description: Optional link label for a downloadable deck or capability document, e.g. 'Open Research capability deck'.

footerLine.description: Footer copyright / provenance line (e.g. 'Selected Work · FY2024–FY2026 · Outcomes owner-verified · 2026-06-22').

hero.headline: We ship outcomes, not slide decks.
hero.standfirst: Seven engagements in two years across Tier-1 banking and insurance clients. Every number on this page is owner-verified and auditable. Bring the outcome you need to move — we will scope it, resource it, and deliver it.

cases.title: Core Banking Re-architecture (GEN2)
cases.description: Full re-platform of the core ledger from a mainframe monolith to a cloud-native microservice architecture, delivered to zero-incident cutover.

cases.title: Real-time Payments Data Platform
cases.description: Governed lakehouse and streaming analytics for real-time payments processing, replacing a batch-overnight pipeline.

cases.title: ML Fraud Detection at Scale
cases.role: ML Platform Lead · Digital Bank · FY25
cases.description: Feature store, real-time inference, and MLOps platform for the fraud detection model suite, with drift monitoring and champion/challenger rollout.

cases.title: Insurance Claims Automation
cases.description: End-to-end claims triage and settlement automation using document AI and a rules engine, cutting manual touch from 74% of claims to 12%.

cases.title: Regulatory Reporting Automation
cases.description: Twenty-two regulatory filings automated end-to-end with a reusable controls library and a full audit trail, retiring manual spreadsheets.

capabilities.label: Core platform delivery
capabilities.description: Tier-1 re-architecture, cutover and parallel-run — measured to zero-incident handover.

capabilities.label: Data & analytics
capabilities.description: Governed lakehouse, lineage and self-serve analytics with cost held down.

capabilities.label: ML & real-time systems
capabilities.description: Feature stores, streaming inference and MLOps with drift and rollout controls.

capabilities.label: Risk, controls & RegTech
capabilities.description: Automated filings, controls libraries and audit trails that pass review first time.

contact.ctaHeadline: Have an outcome in mind? Let's scope it.
contact.ctaBody: Bring the number you need to move. We'll come back with a shaped engagement, the figures it should hit, and references you can call.
